This clarification follows President Trump's proclamation introducing significant changes to the H-1B visa program, raising concerns about its potential impact on America's tech talent pipeline.]]></description><link>https://timesofindia.indiatimes.com/business/india-business/no-need-to-rush-back-big-clarification-on-new-h-1b-rules-what-us-official-said/articleshow/124017894.cms</link><guid>https://timesofindia.indiatimes.com/business/india-business/no-need-to-rush-back-big-clarification-on-new-h-1b-rules-what-us-official-said/articleshow/124017894.cms</guid><pubDate>Sat, 20 Sep 2025 21:39:34 +0530</pubDate><dc:creator>TOI BUSINESS DESK</dc:creator><enclosure type="image/jpeg" length="44420" url="https://static.toiimg.com/photo/msid-124017901,imgsize-44420.cms"/></item><item><title>H-1B fee hike: Trump torpedos visa programme; throws million of Indians into turmoil</title><description><![CDATA[President Trump's executive order imposing a $100,000 fee for H1-B visas has sparked turmoil, impacting numerous Indian professionals. The move, intended to prioritize American jobs, faces legal challenges and criticism for potentially hindering innovation. Companies now weigh the cost of retaining foreign talent against hiring domestic workers, as immigration attorneys prepare to contest the new rules.]]></description><link>https://timesofindia.indiatimes.com/business/india-business/trump-torpedos-h-1b-visa-program-throwing-million-indians-into-turmoil/articleshow/124017958.cms</link><guid>https://timesofindia.indiatimes.com/business/india-business/trump-torpedos-h-1b-visa-program-throwing-million-indians-into-turmoil/articleshow/124017958.cms</guid><pubDate>Sat, 20 Sep 2025 21:43:19 +0530</pubDate><dc:creator>CHIDANAND RAJGHATTA</dc:creator><enclosure type="image/jpeg" length="41496" url="https://static.toiimg.com/photo/msid-124017955,imgsize-41496.cms"/></item><item><title>'Humanitarian consequences': India reacts to US H-1B visa fee hike; maps way forward</title><description><![CDATA[The Indian government has voiced concerns over the US decision to increase H-1B visa fees to $100,000 annually, foreseeing potential disruptions for Indian professionals and their families. This move, effective September 21, 2025, is defended by US officials as a measure to curb program abuse and generate revenue, but it raises worries about its impact on talent mobility and the competitiveness of the US technology sector.]]></description><link>https://timesofindia.indiatimes.com/business/india-business/humanitarian-consequences-india-reacts-to-us-h-1b-visa-fee-hike-maps-way-forward/articleshow/124016383.cms</link><guid>https://timesofindia.indiatimes.com/business/india-business/humanitarian-consequences-india-reacts-to-us-h-1b-visa-fee-hike-maps-way-forward/articleshow/124016383.cms</guid><pubDate>Sat, 20 Sep 2025 19:04:34 +0530</pubDate><dc:creator>TOI BUSINESS DESK</dc:creator><enclosure type="image/jpeg" length="1269542" url="https://static.toiimg.com/photo/msid-124016569,imgsize-1269542.cms"/></item><item><title>Silence of the Lambs: Tech titans tongue-tied over Trump's H-1B move</title><description><![CDATA[Tech giants previously championed H-1B visas. Elon Musk even threatened action to defend it. However, a White House decision changed the landscape. MAGA activists, opposing H1B visas, found support in Stephen Miller. They lobbied for restrictions, targeting the OPT program. US Tech Workers forum celebrated this victory. They urged further action to protect American workers.]]></description><link>https://timesofindia.indiatimes.com/business/india-business/silence-of-the-lambs-tech-titans-tongue-tied-over-trump-torpedoing-h1b-program/articleshow/124017632.cms</link><guid>https://timesofindia.indiatimes.com/business/india-business/silence-of-the-lambs-tech-titans-tongue-tied-over-trump-torpedoing-h1b-program/articleshow/124017632.cms</guid><pubDate>Sat, 20 Sep 2025 21:26:55 +0530</pubDate><dc:creator>CHIDANAND RAJGHATTA</dc:creator><enclosure type="image/jpeg" length="55130" url="https://static.toiimg.com/photo/msid-124018156,imgsize-55130.cms"/></item><item><title>Asia Cup: Pakistan team brings in motivational speaker ahead of India clash</title><description><![CDATA[Following a significant defeat against India and a subsequent handshake controversy, Pakistan's cricket team has enlisted motivational speaker Dr. Raheel Karim. He will assist the team in managing pressure ahead of their crucial Super 4s stage rematch this Sunday. The Pakistan Cricket Board hopes Dr. Karim's experience will bolster the team's morale and performance.]]></description><link>https://timesofindia.indiatimes.com/sports/cricket/asia-cup/asia-cup-pakistan-team-brings-in-motivational-speaker-ahead-of-india-clash/articleshow/124017149.cms</link><guid>https://timesofindia.indiatimes.com/sports/cricket/asia-cup/asia-cup-pakistan-team-brings-in-motivational-speaker-ahead-of-india-clash/articleshow/124017149.cms</guid><pubDate>Sat, 20 Sep 2025 20:54:39 +0530</pubDate><dc:creator>TOI SPORTS DESK</dc:creator><enclosure type="image/jpeg" length="85666" url="https://static.toiimg.com/photo/msid-124017402,imgsize-85666.cms"/></item><item><title>H-1B visa hike: JPMorgan asks staff to return to US before Sept 21</title><description><![CDATA[JPMorgan has advised H1-B visa holders abroad to return to the US before September 21. This follows President Trump's order of a $100,000 annual fee for each H-1B worker. Microsoft has issued a similar advisory. The new rule is expected to impact Indian IT firms like Infosys and TCS.]]></description><link>https://timesofindia.indiatimes.com/technology/tech-news/americas-largest-bank-jpmorgan-to-employees-as-h1-b-visa-fees-increased-to-100000-return-to-us-before-september-21/articleshow/124010959.cms</link><guid>https://timesofindia.indiatimes.com/technology/tech-news/americas-largest-bank-jpmorgan-to-employees-as-h1-b-visa-fees-increased-to-100000-return-to-us-before-september-21/articleshow/124010959.cms</guid><pubDate>Sat, paying</title><description><![CDATA[President Trump's new executive order imposes a $100,000 fee on many H-1B visa applications, impacting companies hiring foreign workers. Exemptions exist for healthcare and engineering roles. The order grants the Homeland Security Secretary power to waive fees for vital national interest roles. Concerns persist that the H-1B program displaces American workers, prompting stricter requirements for highly skilled applicants.]]></description><link>https://timesofindia.indiatimes.com/technology/tech-news/h1-b-visa-price-hike-applicants-who-will-be-exempted-from-paying-100000-fee/articleshow/124015599.cms</link><guid>https://timesofindia.indiatimes.com/technology/tech-news/h1-b-visa-price-hike-applicants-who-will-be-exempted-from-paying-100000-fee/articleshow/124015599.cms</guid><pubDate>Sat, 20 Sep 2025 17:49:15 +0530</pubDate><dc:creator>TOI TECH DESK</dc:creator><enclosure type="image/jpeg" length="32658" url="https://static.toiimg.com/photo/msid-124015571,imgsize-32658.cms"/></item><item><title>'Our biggest enemy is ...': PM Modi's 'atmanirbhar' push amid Trump tariffs</title><description><![CDATA[]]></description><link>https://timesofindia.indiatimes.com/india/main-enemy-of-india-is-dependence-on-other-nations-pm-modis-atmanirbhar-push-amid-trump-tariffs-calls-it-medicine/articleshow/124011894.cms</link><guid>https://timesofindia.indiatimes.com/india/main-enemy-of-india-is-dependence-on-other-nations-pm-modis-atmanirbhar-push-amid-trump-tariffs-calls-it-medicine/articleshow/124011894.cms</guid><pubDate>Sat, 20 Sep 2025 12:49:50 +0530</pubDate><dc:creator>TOI NEWS DESK</dc:creator><enclosure type="image/jpeg" length="1085771" url="https://static.toiimg.com/photo/msid-124011872,imgsize-1085771.cms"/></item><item><title>Congress vs ECI: Karnataka govt forms SIT; to probe 'vote deletion' on Aland seat</title><description><![CDATA[The Karnataka government has formed a Special Investigation Team. This SIT will investigate alleged vote deletions in the Aland assembly segment. The move follows Rahul Gandhi's accusations against the Chief Election Commissioner. Gandhi claimed the CEC protected those deleting votes. The Election Commission refuted Gandhi's claims. The EC stated no online deletion by the public is possible.]]></description><link>https://timesofindia.indiatimes.com/india/congress-vs-eci-karnataka-govt-forms-sit-to-probe-vote-deletion-on-aland-seat/articleshow/124017103.cms</link><guid>https://timesofindia.indiatimes.com/india/congress-vs-eci-karnataka-govt-forms-sit-to-probe-vote-deletion-on-aland-seat/articleshow/124017103.cms</guid><pubDate>Sat, 20 Sep 2025 20:25:28 +0530</pubDate><dc:creator>TOI NEWS DESK</dc:creator><enclosure type="image/jpeg" length="74056" url="https://static.toiimg.com/photo/msid-124017173,imgsize-74056.cms"/></item><item><title>In numbers: H-1B was already tough for Indians; Trump's $100K fee just made it tougher</title><description><![CDATA[The H-1B programme was created in 1990 with a cap of 65,000 visas per year, later expanded to include 20,000 extra slots for US master’s degree holders. That total of 85,000 visas has not changed in over a decade, even as America’s economy and demand for skilled workers exploded.]]></description><link>https://timesofindia.indiatimes.com/world/us/in-numbers-h-1b-was-already-tough-for-indians-donald-trumps-100000-fee-just-made-it-tougher/articleshow/124014678.cms</link><guid>https://timesofindia.indiatimes.com/world/us/in-numbers-h-1b-was-already-tough-for-indians-donald-trumps-100000-fee-just-made-it-tougher/articleshow/124014678.cms</guid><pubDate>Sat, 20 Sep 2025 18:40:07 +0530</pubDate><dc:creator>TOI WORLD DESK</dc:creator><enclosure type="image/jpeg" length="98938" url="https://static.toiimg.com/photo/msid-124016091,imgsize-98938.cms"/></item><item><title>H-1B visa fees hike to $100K: Microsoft warns employees; asks to return immediately</title><description><![CDATA[Microsoft urged its H-1B and H-4 visa employees to return to the US before the September 21 deadline due to a new $100,000 annual fee per H-1B worker. The company also advised those already in the US to remain there.]]></description><link>https://timesofindia.indiatimes.com/technology/tech-news/microsoft-has-a-24-hour-deadline-warning-for-indian-and-other-foreign-employees-after-h1-b-visa-fees-hike-to-100000-strongly-recommend-h1b-visa-holders-/articleshow/124010245.cms</link><guid>https://timesofindia.indiatimes.com/technology/tech-news/microsoft-has-a-24-hour-deadline-warning-for-indian-and-other-foreign-employees-after-h1-b-visa-fees-hike-to-100000-strongly-recommend-h1b-visa-holders-/articleshow/124010245.cms</guid><pubDate>Sat, 20 Sep 2025 10:10:31 +0530</pubDate><dc:creator>TOI TECH DESK</dc:creator><enclosure type="image/jpeg" length="1472123" url="https://static.toiimg.com/photo/msid-124010226,imgsize-1472123.cms"/></item><item><title>PM's salute to young boy wins hearts, viral video captures priceless reaction - watch</title><description><![CDATA[]]></description><link>https://timesofindia.indiatimes.com/india/pm-modis-salute-to-young-boy-in-bhavnagar-wins-hearts-viral-video-captures-priceless-reaction-watch/articleshow/124014315.cms</link><guid>https://timesofindia.indiatimes.com/india/pm-modis-salute-to-young-boy-in-bhavnagar-wins-hearts-viral-video-captures-priceless-reaction-watch/articleshow/124014315.cms</guid><pubDate>Sat, 20 Sep 2025 16:13:35 +0530</pubDate><dc:creator>TOI NEWS DESK</dc:creator><enclosure type="image/jpeg" length="152344" url="https://static.toiimg.com/photo/msid-124014307,imgsize-152344.cms"/></item><item><title>Asia Cup: Drama in Dubai! This follows a dispute with match referee Andy Pycroft over a handshake controversy during their previous encounter. The PCB's request to remove Pycroft was rejected, leading to heightened tensions and potential ICC action against Pakistan.]]></description><link>https://timesofindia.indiatimes.com/sports/cricket/asia-cup/drama-continues-in-dubai-pakistan-cancel-press-conference-ahead-of-india-clash/articleshow/124015485.cms</link><guid>https://timesofindia.indiatimes.com/sports/cricket/asia-cup/drama-continues-in-dubai-pakistan-cancel-press-conference-ahead-of-india-clash/articleshow/124015485.cms</guid><pubDate>Sat, 20 Sep 2025 17:55:27 +0530</pubDate><dc:creator>TOI SPORTS DESK</dc:creator><enclosure type="image/jpeg" length="115408" url="https://static.toiimg.com/photo/msid-124015614,imgsize-115408.cms"/></item><item><title>NFL: Gracie Hunt shares heartfelt update with boyfriend amid Chiefs skid</title><description><![CDATA[Gracie Hunt, the Kansas City Chiefs' heiress, shared heartwarming photos on Instagram from a wedding she attended with her boyfriend, Derek Green. Along with thoughtful design details, she creates a warm, welcoming atmosphere.]]></description><link>https://timesofindia.indiatimes.com/sports/nhl/news/nhl-star-connor-mcdavids-wife-lauren-kyle-opens-her-cottage-retreat/articleshow/123998930.cms</link><guid>https://timesofindia.indiatimes.com/sports/nhl/news/nhl-star-connor-mcdavids-wife-lauren-kyle-opens-her-cottage-retreat/articleshow/123998930.cms</guid><pubDate>Fri, 19 Sep 2025 19:00:00 +0530</pubDate><dc:creator>GLOBAL SPORTS DESK</dc:creator><enclosure type="image/jpeg" length="22644" url="https://static.toiimg.com/photo/msid-123998912,imgsize-22644.cms"/></item><item><title>Charlie Kirk’s assassination: Erika Kirk pulls Taylor Swift into controversy</title><description><![CDATA[Following Charlie Kirk's shocking assassination, his widow, Erika Kirk, has ignited controversy by involving Taylor Swift in memorial plans. Despite Trump's touted "deal," experts note finalization is distant.]]></description><link>https://timesofindia.indiatimes.com/business/international-business/open-non-discriminatory-china-on-tiktok-deal-with-us-urges-fair-business-environment/articleshow/124016559.cms</link><guid>https://timesofindia.indiatimes.com/business/international-business/open-non-discriminatory-china-on-tiktok-deal-with-us-urges-fair-business-environment/articleshow/124016559.cms</guid><pubDate>Sat, 20 Sep 2025 19:40:46 +0530</pubDate><dc:creator>TOI BUSINESS DESK</dc:creator><enclosure type="image/jpeg" length="28720" url="https://static.toiimg.com/photo/msid-124016758,imgsize-28720.cms"/></item><item><title>‘Gukesh is not even 20 yet’: Grand Swiss was an eye-opener in many ways</title><description><![CDATA[The FIDE Grand Swiss 2025 witnessed Anish Giri's triumph and Vaishali Rameshbabu's repeat victory, alongside Matthias Bluebaum's unexpected Candidates qualification. Despite Gukesh Dommaraju's stumble, the event highlighted India's mixed fortunes and the rise of young talents like America's Abhimanyu Mishra. Divya Deshmukh's fearless performance in the Open section also underscored the growing strength of women in chess, inspiring future participation.]]></description><link>https://timesofindia.indiatimes.com/sports/chess/chess-d-gukesh-is-not-even-20-yet-fide-grand-swiss-2025-was-an-eye-opener-in-many-ways/articleshow/124013864.cms</link><guid>https://timesofindia.indiatimes.com/sports/chess/chess-d-gukesh-is-not-even-20-yet-fide-grand-swiss-2025-was-an-eye-opener-in-many-ways/articleshow/124013864.cms</guid><pubDate>Sat, 20 Sep 2025 16:47:34 +0530</pubDate><dc:creator>JYOTIRMOY HALDER</dc:creator><enclosure type="image/jpeg" length="81870" url="https://static.toiimg.com/photo/msid-124014596,imgsize-81870.cms"/></item><item><title>H-1B fee hike: Trump’s $100,000 rule sparks outcry; attorneys prepare legal action</title><description><![CDATA[Donald Trump's new rule impacts H-1B visa holders. A $100,000 fee is needed for entry after September 21, 2025. This affects many Indians awaiting US jobs. Legal challenges are expected. The policy raises concerns about fairness. Experts predict uncertainty for businesses and professionals. The proclamation's legality is under scrutiny. It may face court injunctions soon.]]></description><link>https://timesofindia.indiatimes.com/world/us/trumps-100000-pay-to-play-h-1b-visa-rule-sparks-outcry-immigration-attorneys-gear-up-to-file-suits/articleshow/124014488.cms</link><guid>https://timesofindia.indiatimes.com/world/us/trumps-100000-pay-to-play-h-1b-visa-rule-sparks-outcry-immigration-attorneys-gear-up-to-file-suits/articleshow/124014488.cms</guid><pubDate>Sat, 20 Sep 2025 16:26:41 +0530</pubDate><dc:creator>LUBNA KABLY</dc:creator><enclosure type="image/jpeg" length="46378" url="https://static.toiimg.com/photo/msid-124014472,imgsize-46378.cms"/></item><item><title>Stephen A Smith vs Jimmy Kimmel: 'Where was the joke?' Smith criticized Kimmel's comments about President Trump's reaction to Kirk's death. India captain explains</title><description><![CDATA[Suryakumar Yadav revealed he closes his eyes during the Indian national anthem to reflect on the immense pride and gratitude he feels representing his country. In most cases, is caused by lifestyle factors and eating habits and can be relieved without the help of medication. Severe or persistent constipation can signal underlying medical conditions, in such cases it is advised that individuals seek medical advice. ]]></description><link>https://timesofindia.indiatimes.com/life-style/health-fitness/health-news/what-causes-constipation-5-underlying-reasons-that-can-be-tackled-easily/articleshow/123999564.cms</link><guid>https://timesofindia.indiatimes.com/life-style/health-fitness/health-news/what-causes-constipation-5-underlying-reasons-that-can-be-tackled-easily/articleshow/123999564.cms</guid><pubDate>Sat, 20 Sep 2025 11:00:00 +0530</pubDate><dc:creator>TOI LIFESTYLE DESK</dc:creator><enclosure type="image/jpeg" length="27642" url="https://static.toiimg.com/photo/msid-123999589,imgsize-27642.cms"/></item><item><title>Cyberattack at European airports; flights delayed, cancelled - all you need to know</title><description><![CDATA[A cyberattack on a third-party service provider has caused significant disruptions at major European airports, including Heathrow, Brussels, and Berlin. Automated check-in and boarding systems were rendered inoperable, forcing airlines to switch to manual processing, leading to widespread flight delays and cancellations. Airports are working to resolve the issue, while Frankfurt Airport confirmed it was unaffected.]]></description><link>https://timesofindia.indiatimes.com/world/europe/cyberattack-hits-european-airports-check-in-systems-down-at-heathrow-brussels-berlin-triggers-flight-delays-cancellations/articleshow/124013016.cms</link><guid>https://timesofindia.indiatimes.com/world/europe/cyberattack-hits-european-airports-check-in-systems-down-at-heathrow-brussels-berlin-triggers-flight-delays-cancellations/articleshow/124013016.cms</guid><pubDate>Sat, 20 Sep 2025 14:13:28 +0530</pubDate><dc:creator>TOI WORLD DESK</dc:creator><enclosure type="image/jpeg" length="1183924" url="https://static.toiimg.com/photo/msid-124013021,imgsize-1183924.cms"/></item></channel></rss>

      <title><![CDATA[Valuations of Indian banks within palatable range; quality remains key driver: Jefferies’ Prakhar Sharma]]></title>
      <link><![CDATA[https://www.cnbctv18.com/market/indian-banks-credit-growth-asset-quality-jefferies-prakhar-sharma-19681918.htm]]></link>
      <description><![CDATA[Indian banks are entering a phase of cautious optimism with valuations seen as reasonable and quality emerging as the key differentiator, says Jefferies’ Prakhar Sharma. Credit and deposit growth are steady at around 10%, aided by policy support, while asset quality concerns appear temporary. “Good, high-quality stocks that deliver high teens RoEs are trading between 15–16 times earnings… in some cases even at a discount to large global banks,” Sharma told CNBC-TV18.]]></description>
      <pubDate><![CDATA[Sat, 20 Sep 2025 16:44:19 +0530]]></pubDate>
      <dc:creator>Swapnil Deshpande</dc:creator>

    </item>

      <description><![CDATA[On the occasion of Prime Minister Narendra Modi’s 75th birthday, experts have praised his distinctive leadership style and long-term vision for India. R Balasubramaniam highlighted Modi’s rare ability to balance immediate priorities with future national goals, while Baba Kalyani lauded his push for defence manufacturing, innovation, and strong international relationships. Both emphasised that reforms in science, technology, and innovation will define Modi’s legacy as India works toward becoming a developed nation by 2047.]]></description>

    <item>
      <title><![CDATA[Bank chiefs see credit growth reviving to 11–13% despite Q1 slowdown]]></title>
      <link><![CDATA[https://www.cnbctv18.com/business/banks-credit-deposit-growth-outlook-banking-transformation-summit-19676963.htm]]></link>
      <description><![CDATA[Top bankers at the Banking Transformation Summit struck an optimistic note on credit growth, projecting 11–13% expansion for the industry despite a tepid first quarter. SBI’s Rama Mohan Rao Amara said corporate demand was subdued as companies tapped bond markets, but expected GST reforms, a good monsoon and tax cuts to revive borrowing. Bank of Baroda’s Debadatta Chand was most bullish at 13–15%, while others stayed in the 11–12% range. Bank chiefs also stressed that deposit growth would be led by retail, agriculture and MSMEs, and reaffirmed their commitment to infrastructure lending despite a shift in household savings toward markets.]]></description>
      <pubDate><![CDATA[Tue, 16 Sep 2025 22:10:58 +0530]]></pubDate>

      <title><![CDATA[No plan to dilute majority govt ownership in PSU banks: DFS Secretary M Nagaraju]]></title>
      <link><![CDATA[https://www.cnbctv18.com/economy/psu-banks-government-ownership-consolidation-capital-raising-dfs-secretary-nagaraju-19676929.htm]]></link>
      <description><![CDATA[The government has no plan to dilute majority ownership in public sector banks, Financial Services Secretary M Nagaraju told CNBC-TV18, stressing their role in serving underserved sectors. He said PSU banks will remain majority-owned by the government, while also highlighting the need for both more banks and bigger banks to meet India’s growing financial needs. Consolidation, he added, is a long-term goal with the vision set for 2047, as the government also focuses on capital raising to support lending.]]></description>

      <description><![CDATA[The GST exemption on individual and family floater insurance policies is set to give a strong push to retail insurance, with industry players expecting it to improve penetration and expand coverage. Satish Gidugu, CEO of Medi Assist, said the change will ease affordability pressures and bridge the coverage gap, though the full 18% savings may not pass through to policyholders. While group policies remain taxed, insurers are exploring hybrid products, and Gidugu noted that lower GST on life-saving drugs and medical equipment could further reduce healthcare costs.]]></description>

    <item>
      <title><![CDATA[GST 2.0: Key features and what change for businesses and consumers]]></title>
      <link><![CDATA[https://www.cnbctv18.com/business/finance/gst-2-tax-reforms-change-businesses-consumers-from-september-22-features-19672551.htm]]></link>
      <description><![CDATA[The biggest change in GST 2.0 is the rationalisation of tax slabs. Instead of four main rates—5%, 12%, 18%, and 28%—the new system has just three: 5% on essentials, 18% on most goods and services, and 40% on luxury and “sin” goods such as tobacco, aerated drinks, and pan masala.]]></description>
      <pubDate><![CDATA[Fri, 12 Sep 2025 15:20:05 +0530]]></pubDate>
      <dc:creator>Puja Menon</dc:creator>
      <guid><![CDATA[https://www.cnbctv18.com/business/finance/gst-2-tax-reforms-change-businesses-consumers-from-september-22-features-19672551.htm]]></guid>
      <category><![CDATA[Business]]></category>
      <media:content height="675" width="1200" medium="image" url="https://images.cnbctv18.com/uploads/2025/02/shutterstock-1903314829-2025-02-2dc1254ced886a982ac4c2838375f987-scaled.jpg"/>
    </item>

      <description><![CDATA[In this episode of Leadership Language, Sanjeev Bikhchandani, the father of Indian startups and Padma Shri awardee, shares candid insights from his extraordinary journey—from launching Naukri.com in his father’s garage to backing over 140 iconic startups like Zomato and Policybazaar. He reflects on the highs and lows of entrepreneurship, the importance of first-mover advantage, customer validation, and resilience in the face of setbacks. With his signature blend of honesty, pragmatism, and ingenuity, Bikhchandani offers timeless lessons for young entrepreneurs and investors on risk management, spotting opportunities, and building lasting value.]]></description>

      <link><![CDATA[https://www.cnbctv18.com/business/india-reit-yields-diversification-logistics-data-centres-growth-19673206.htm]]></link>
      <description><![CDATA[India’s REIT market has moved well past its experimental phase. A new ANAROCK–CREDAI report pegs the sector at a market cap of $18 billion as of August 2025, with projections to cross $25 billion by 2030. Strong office absorption, yield premiums of 6–7%, and rising global capital inflows underpin the growth story. Yet only 20% of India’s institutional-grade real estate is listed under REITs, leaving significant headroom — particularly in southern metros.]]></description>
